steps: A system has no solution when the lines are parallel: same slope but different y-intercepts (coefficients proportional, constants not proportional).
Check each pair: multiply one equation by a scalar and compare both sides to the other equation.
Choice D: multiply $-2x+3y=9$ by $-2$ to get $4x-6y=-18$. The second equation is $4x-6y=18$. Left sides match but $-18 \neq 18$ — parallel lines, no solution.
Choices A and C produce identical equations (infinite solutions); Choice B has non-proportional coefficients (one solution).
choiceAnalysis:
• Choice A — '$-2x+3y=-9$ and $2x-3y=9$': Multiply the first by $-1$: $2x-3y=9$, identical to the second. Infinitely many solutions. ✗
  • Choice B — '$2x-3y=9$ and $3x+4y=10$': Slopes are $\frac{2}{3}$ and $-\frac{3}{4}$ — not proportional, so the lines intersect at one point. ✗
  • Choice C — '$2x-3y=9$ and $-6x+9y=-27$': Multiply the first by $-3$: $-6x+9y=-27$, identical to the second. Infinitely many solutions. ✗
  • Choice D — '$-2x+3y=9$ and $4x-6y=18$': Multiply the first by $-2$: $4x-6y=-18$. The second gives $4x-6y=18$. Same left side, $-18 \neq 18$ — parallel lines, no solution. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
